project.objective: Who were the parents of William Henry Bottemiller, born 29 December 1862 in Franconia Township, Chisago County, Minnesota, and died 13 May 1929 in Oregon City, Clackamas County, Oregon?

questions.question: Who are the parents of William Henry Bottemiller, born 29 December 1862 in Franconia Township, Chisago County, Minnesota?
questions.rationale: This is the core question of the research objective. William Henry Bottemiller (249W-9LP) has no parent links in tree.gedcomx.json. He was born in 1862 in Franconia Township, Chisago County, Minnesota — a Swedish Lutheran settlement area — and by 1880 resided in Bertha, Todd County, Minnesota, suggesting a family westward migration. The 1870 census (age 7) should place him in a parental household in Chisago County; the 1880 census (age 17) may show him still with family in Todd County. His 1929 Oregon death certificate should name his parents. Minnesota church and state birth records for Chisago County may also document the parentage directly.

items.rationale: William Henry Bottemiller died 13 May 1929 in Oregon City, Clackamas County. His Oregon death certificate (Oregon State Archives Death Records, 1864–1968, col. 3657650) is the single most direct source for his parents: Oregon certificates from this era include fields for the father's name and mother's maiden name. An informant — likely a surviving child — provided these details. The existing FamilySearch profile sources (ARK 1:1:F8WM-X8F, 1:1:VZHS-YXF) indicate the record is accessible; this item retrieves the full-image certificate to extract the parents' fields.

In June 1870 William was approximately 7 years old and almost certainly living in the parental household in Franconia Township, Chisago County — the same location given for his 1862 birth. The household head (father) and his wife (mother) will be named. Ages of all household members will help identify siblings and estimate parents' birth years and origins. This is the earliest indexed federal census placing William with his family.

The Minnesota State Census of 1865 (col. 1503054, 246K indexed records) was taken when William was about 2. It should place him with both parents in Chisago County. State censuses enumerate household members with age and sex; the head's name is recorded and the family structure confirms parentage. This is earlier than the 1870 federal census and could surface information predating any family migration.

The 1880 federal census places William (age 17) in Bertha, Todd County, Minnesota (confirmed by tree.gedcomx.json). The family may have migrated northwest from Chisago County between 1875 and 1880. If William is still in a parental household, this record names both father and mother and gives their birthplaces (including parents' birthplaces by state/country in the 1880 schedule). Even if William had left home, locating a co-resident Bottemiller family of the right generation in Todd County advances the parentage hypothesis.

The Minnesota State Census of 1875 (col. 1503053, 612K indexed records) was taken when William was about 12. It bridges the 1870 federal and 1880 federal censuses and can reveal when the family migrated from Chisago County to Todd County. It names the household head, enumerated members, and their ages, providing corroboration for the father's identity identified in earlier censuses.

FamilySearch 'Minnesota, Births and Christenings, 1840–1980' (col. 1680827) and 'Minnesota, Birth and Death Records, 1866–1916' (col. 1858352) may contain a birth or christening record for William or his siblings born to the same parents in Chisago or Todd County. A birth record for any sibling would directly name both parents and confirm the family unit, even if William's own 1862 record is absent from these collections.

Per GPS parentage-question standards, the parents' marriage is a required dedicated plan item. Minnesota County Marriages 1853–1983 (col. 1803974, 946K indexed) covers Chisago County from its earliest settlement. The marriage record of William's parents (likely predating his 29 December 1862 birth) supplies: (a) the father's full name; (b) the mother's maiden name, which census and death records typically omit; and (c) a date prior to William's birth constituting direct paternity evidence. This evidence is critical to identifying the mother, who may appear in other records only under her maiden name.

items.jurisdiction: Chisago County and Todd County, Minnesota
items.rationale: The surname Bottemiller derives from German or Swiss 'Bodenmüller.' Early settlers in Chisago County included not only Swedish Lutherans but also German, Swiss, and other immigrant communities. FamilySearch 'Minnesota, Church Records, 1798–1991' (col. 2790243) contains baptism, confirmation, and membership records from various denominations. A baptismal entry for William or a sibling would directly name both parents. Supplementary index: Dalby Database Minnesota Church Records (dalbydata.com).

If William's father died in Minnesota — either in Chisago County before 1880 or in Todd County after the family's migration — a will or estate administration may name William as a son and heir. FamilySearch 'Minnesota, Will Records, 1849–1985' (col. 1607922, 189K indexed) covers both counties. A will naming William Bottemiller as a legatee is direct evidence of the father-son relationship and may also identify siblings. Also searchable on Ancestry as 'Minnesota Wills and Probate Records, 1801-1925.'

Bottemiller is a rare and distinctive surname unlikely to appear frequently in Minnesota records. A full-text co-occurrence search across the FamilySearch corpus (via the search-full-text skill) for 'Bottemiller' may surface church registers, newspaper items, local history mentions, or unindexed records not captured by name-indexed searches. Variant spellings (Bottenmiller, Bodemiller, Bodenmiller) should be tried. Execute via the search-full-text skill.

query.note: Initial search used Bottemiller/William/col.3657650 — zero results; retried as Battemiller/Henry (name as indexed in tree sources) without collection filter

log.notes: Found two records for subject: ark:F8WM-X8F (Oregon Deaths and Burials 1903-1947, col.1675532, matchScore 0.94) and ark:VZHS-YXF (Oregon Death Index 1903-1998, col.1946790, matchScore 0.83). Both already attached to 249W-9LP. The Deaths and Burials record names father as 'Casper A. Battermiller' and mother as 'Mary Mehlman' — direct parentage evidence from the death certificate informant.

Oregon State Archives Death Records col. 3657650 does not index this record under 'Bottemiller/William'. Tried variant 'Battemiller/Henry' (log_003) — also no match for subject. Certificate found only in cols. 1675532 and 1946790 (log_001). Col. 3657650 may carry an image-only entry or transcribed name differently still.
Single result was 'Will Bottomly' in Martin County — unrelated English family. No Bottemiller/Battermiller child of this name and birth year found in Minnesota 1870 census.

Nil. Father Casper Battermiller/Bottemiller not found as head of household in 1870 Minnesota census. All three 1870 census searches exhausted; surname may be unindexed or transcribed beyond recognition in this collection.

Top match: 'Willie Bottermiller' (matchScore 0.95, 5-star, already attached to 249W-9LP) in household of Henry Bottermiller, Bertha, Todd County, MN. Father = Henry Bottermiller, born 1828 Germany; mother = Mary Bottermiller, born 1838 Germany. Father's name conflicts with Oregon death cert ('Casper A. Battermiller'). ark:/61903/1:1:MZ9R-28C.

Nil. A second search dropping the bride surname also returned no Bottermiller/Battermiller marriages in Minnesota county records. Parents Henry and Mary were both born in Germany (~1828, ~1838); first child born 1860 in MN. Marriage almost certainly occurred in Germany before emigration and would not appear in Minnesota records.

No Minnesota birth record for William found. Top results are Oregon birth records of William's own children (where he appears as father, 5-star match to 249W-9LP). Minnesota did not require mandatory civil birth registration until 1900; an 1862 birth in Chisago County was almost certainly not registered.

Found Minnesota probate record (~1881, ark:/61903/3:1:3Q9M-C9T9-6TCW) naming 'Henry Boltermiller / Henry Battermiller / Henry Bottemiller' as witness — corroborates 1880 census father name 'Henry.' Also found 1885 Wadena County MN deed for Charles Battermiller of Todd County (eldest son, born ~1860 MN). The Oregon death cert FTS transcription shows 'Casper A. Battermiller' as father and 'Mary Mealman' as mother. Full-text search for +Bottermiller (1855-1910) returned 0 results.

query.note: Also searched givenName=Henry; both nil. Henry also not found in 1900 MN census.

log.notes: No probate records for Bottermiller/Battermiller in Minnesota. Henry Bottermiller also not found in 1900 MN census — may have died 1880-1900. FTS mention of Henry as witness in MN probate (~1881) not accessible via record_read (image-only 3:1: ARK).

Found William Battemiller (ark:/61903/1:1:SPSM-N8C) in household of Henry Battemiller, Franconia, Chisago County, MN 1865 — William's birthplace, 3 years after his birth. Corroborates Henry as father.

Found William Bottermiller (ark:/61903/1:1:MQFJ-ZZB), born 1862 MN, in household of Henry Bottermiller, Bertha, Todd County, MN 1885. pli_005 targeted 1875 census; 1885 is the next available MN state census. Confirms family still together and Henry as head.

Nil. Henry Battemiller not found in 1875 MN State Census (col. 1503053). Family may have been mid-migration from Chisago County (1865) to Todd County (1880) in 1875, or indexed under a different surname variant. 1885 MN census used as substitute for pli_005.

Nil variant. Family not indexed in 1875 MN State Census under either Battemiller or Bottermiller. The 1875 census would have been key to identifying which wife (Anna or Mary) was present between the 1865 and 1880 enumerations; its absence means c_002 (mother identity) cannot be resolved via this source without image browsing.

Nil. Henry Battermiller not found in MN 1870 federal census. Combined with prior searches under William (log_003) and Casper (log_004), the family is not locatable in any 1870 census — likely transcribed beyond recognition or missed by the enumerator. pli_002 exhausted.

Nil for Battemiller and Bottermiller in MN Church Records collection 2790243. No baptism or marriage record for the family found in this indexed collection. Church records for German immigrant communities in Chisago and Todd Counties may not be indexed here; unindexed image series would require a volume_search and browse approach.

query.purpose: Retrieve 1865 MN census Battemiller household to confirm wife given name and locate original image ARK for c_002 resolution

log.notes: Indexed record confirms wife given name as 'Anna Battemiller' — consistent with a_021. Official FamilySearch source citation reads: 'Entry for Henry Battemiller and Anna Battemiller, 1865.' Household members indexed: Henry (head), Anna (wife), Augustus, Eliza, Charles, William, Fred, Mary J. Original image ARK located: ark:/61903/3:1:939Z-YPF8-9 (1.8 MB — exceeds automated read transport limit). Handwritten original requires human review at familysearch.org to definitively confirm spelling. The indexed evidence leans strongly toward Anna as genuine, making the two-wife hypothesis (Anna died before 1880; Henry remarried Mary Mehlman) the most probable reading of the evidence.

assertions.informant_bias_notes: Index reflects the name as reported to the original certificate's personal informant; name order (Henry William) is inverted relative to William Henry, a common transcription/reporting variant.
Death date/place on the underlying certificate is the physician's official attestation; index does not preserve the physician's name.

Subject's name here ('Henry William Battemiller') is a given-name-order/spelling variant of tree subject 'William Henry Bottemiller' — same underlying person, indexing/transcription variant, not a conflicting identity.

assertions.value: Died 13 May 1929, Clackamas, Oregon, United States

assertions.informant_bias_notes: Birth year relayed by a family informant who did not witness the subject's 1862 birth; per death-certificate doctrine, the decedent's own birth information reported by a not-present family informant is indirect evidence despite appearing as a stated field.

Father's name reported by a family informant not present at the subject's 1862 birth — secondhand parentage information typical of death-certificate informants. This is a single, uncorroborated derivative record; identification of 'Casper A. Battermiller' as father is tentative pending corroboration (e.g., 1870/1880 MN census, MN vital or church records per plan items pli_002-pli_008). Shares the same informant as the mother's-name assertion below (a_005) — together they form one evidence unit under GPS Standard 4, not two independent corroborations.

Mother's maiden name reported by a family informant not present at the subject's 1862 birth — secondhand parentage information. Single, uncorroborated derivative record; tentative pending independent corroboration (e.g., MN marriage record of Casper Battermiller and Mary Mehlman per pli_007). Shares the same informant as the father's-name assertion above (a_004) — one evidence unit, not two independent sources, per GPS Standard 4.

Surviving spouse named as 'Lilly Jane' (no surname given in this index); corresponds to existing tree person Lillie Jane Kleinsmith (9SPC-3H6), the subject's wife per the existing Couple relationship in tree.gedcomx.json.

assertions.value: Residing in Bertha, Todd County, Minnesota, 1880, in the household of head Henry Bottermiller
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Enumerator canvassed/visited the dwelling and recorded the household as observed.

assertions.informant: unknown household member (likely a parent, since Willie was a dependent child in the household)

This is indirect evidence of a parent-child relationship — it corroborates but does not by itself prove Henry and Mary as biological parents of William Henry Bottemiller (249W-9LP) for q_001.

assertions.informant: unknown household member (likely Henry himself, self-reporting as head of household)
assertions.informant_bias_notes: No household member present in 1880 could have witnessed Henry's 1828 birth in Germany, decades before the family's formation in Minnesota; per doctrine a person also cannot provide primary information about their own birth. Birth year is calculated from stated age. NAME CONFLICT: this 1880 census names the head of household 'Henry Bottermiller,' whereas the subject's 1929 Oregon death certificate names the father 'Casper A. Battermiller' — a given-name discrepancy requiring resolution via additional records (parents' marriage record, church records).

Birth year is calculated from stated age. The 1929 Oregon death certificate gives the mother's maiden name as 'Mehlman' (not present in this census, which records only the given name Mary).

assertions.informant_bias_notes: NAME CONFLICT: this record's presumed wife is named 'Anna Battemiller,' but existing tree stub I2 (mother candidate) was built from the 1929 Oregon death certificate naming the mother 'Mary Mehlman' — a different given name, no obvious variant relationship. This is a new, unresolved discrepancy in the mother's identity, parallel to the existing father given-name conflict (c_001), and needs independent corroboration (e.g., 1870/1875/1880 MN census showing the wife's name, or a marriage record) before either name can be preferred as William's mother.
A child could not report own name/birth information to the enumerator; a parent or other adult household member is the likely respondent, though respondent is not specifically recorded.

This matches 249W-9LP (William Henry Bottemiller) on: (1) name — same given name and surname in inverted order, a common indexing/transcription variant; (2) death date — exact match; (3) death place — Clackamas, Oregon, exact match; (4) already attached to 249W-9LP in FamilySearch tree (5-star). matchScore 0.825 from rank_search_matches.

The 'father_of_deceased' persona (p_11207172946) in F8WM-X8F is named 'Casper A. Battermiller.' I1 (Casper A. Battermiller) is the stub created from this record to represent the named father. No independent corroboration yet — a single derivative death-record entry reported by an unnamed family informant. 'Battermiller' is a spelling variant of 'Bottemiller/Battermiller.' conflicts.description: The Oregon death certificate (src_002, F8WM-X8F, filed 1929) names William's father as 'Casper A. Battermiller.' The 1880 U.S. Census (src_003) records the head of the Bottermiller household in Bertha, Todd County, MN — in which Willie Bottermiller (249W-9LP) was enumerated — as 'Henry Bottermiller,' born 1828 in Germany. Both sources refer to the same individual: the surname, German birthplace, farming occupation, and Willie's co-residence are consistent, but the given name differs: 'Casper A.' vs. 'Henry.' Possible explanations: (1) Henry was his first name and Casper (Kaspar) was a middle name used by family members; (2) the 1929 informant had imprecise knowledge of the father's first name; or (3) the 1880 enumerator recorded the name incorrectly.
conflicts.independence_analysis: The four sources naming 'Henry' are independent of each other: the 1865 Minnesota State Census, the 1880 U.S. Federal Census, the 1885 Minnesota State Census, and the circa-1881 Minnesota probate witness reference were compiled by different enumerators and officials at different times (spanning 1865–1885), in different jurisdictions (Chisago County MN, Todd County MN, federal), under different authorities (state and federal). None shares a common informant. The death certificate naming 'Casper A.' stands alone as a single derivative source with a single unidentified family informant.
conflicts.weighing_analysis: Four concordant independent original records with primary or proximate information outweigh one derivative record carrying secondary information from a distant informant. The 1865 census was taken three years after William's birth in the same township; the head-of-household name was recorded by a government enumerator in direct contact with the household. The 1929 death certificate informant was reporting a given name from a 67-year temporal distance for a person who likely predeceased William by decades. No mechanism exists by which four independent officials across twenty years could systematically record the wrong given name for the same person.
conflicts.resolution_rationale: The father's given name is Henry. 'Casper A.' on the 1929 Oregon death certificate is most plausibly an error by the family informant — faulty memory after decades, confusion with a relative's or neighbor's name, or an occasional middle name used in family address. Henry Bottermiller's name has been corrected from 'Casper A. Battermiller' to 'Henry Bottermiller' in tree.gedcomx.json (stub person I1, name N1).

conflicts.description: Mother identity conflict: 'Anna Battemiller' (1865 MN census indexed, a_021; FS citation 'Entry for Henry Battemiller and Anna Battemiller, 1865') vs. 'Mary Bottermiller' (1880 US census a_016, 1885 MN census a_046) vs. 'Mary Mehlman' (1929 OR death cert a_007). UPDATE log_016 (2026-07-12): record_read of ark:/61903/1:1:SPSM-N88 confirms the FamilySearch indexed record names the wife 'Anna.' Original image ark:/61903/3:1:939Z-YPF8-9 exists (1.8 MB) but exceeds automated read threshold — requires human review at familysearch.org. Two-wife hypothesis is the most probable interpretation: Anna = biological mother (present 1865, in William's birth location), Mary Mehlman = second wife/stepmother (present 1880+). Cannot formally resolve without original image confirmation.

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: Searched FamilySearch indexed collections for Chisago County and Todd County, Minnesota (1865, 1875, 1880, 1885 censuses); Oregon death records 1903–1947 and 1903–1998 death index; Minnesota pre-1900 birth registrations (nil — civil registration not in force); FamilySearch full-text search for probate records naming Henry Bottemiller as witness (positive, log_008). Ancestry, MyHeritage, and FindAGrave were not searched. German origin and immigration records were not searched. Post-search follow-up (log_016, 2026-07-12): record_read of ark:/61903/1:1:SPSM-N88 confirmed the FamilySearch indexed record for the 1865 census household names the wife 'Anna Battemiller'; the official source citation reads 'Entry for Henry Battemiller and Anna Battemiller, 1865.' The original register image ARK is ark:/61903/3:1:939Z-YPF8-9 (1.8 MB); it exceeds the automated read threshold and requires direct human review at familysearch.org to confirm or refute the indexer's reading. Research is not declared exhaustive: c_002 remains unresolved pending original image review.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
# Proof Argument: Parents of William Henry Bottemiller (1862–1929)

**Research Question:** Who are the parents of William Henry Bottemiller, born 29 December 1862 in Franconia Township, Chisago County, Minnesota, and died 13 May 1929 in Oregon City, Clackamas County, Oregon (FamilySearch person 249W-9LP)?

**Conclusion (Probable):** The preponderance of evidence strongly indicates that William Henry Bottemiller's father is **Henry Bottermiller**, born approximately 1827–1828 in Germany, a farmer who resided in Franconia Township, Chisago County, Minnesota by 1865 and relocated to Bertha, Todd County, Minnesota by 1880. The identity of William's biological mother is more uncertain. The 1929 Oregon death certificate names **Mary Mehlman** as the mother, and she appears as Henry's wife in 1880 and 1885 census records; however, the 1865 Minnesota State Census — taken just three years after William's birth in his birthplace — names a woman called "Anna" as Henry's wife, raising the probability that Anna was William's biological mother and Mary Mehlman was Henry's second wife.

---

## Evidence

### 1865 Minnesota State Census (Original)

The 1865 Minnesota State Census (original government enumeration; FamilySearch collection 1503054) lists **Henry Battemiller** as head of household in Franconia, Chisago County, Minnesota — the same township and county recorded as William's birthplace on his 1929 death certificate. Among the children enumerated is **William Battemiller**, approximately 2–3 years of age, consistent with a birth date of 29 December 1862 [src_004; a_019, a_033]. Also present: wife "Anna Battemiller" [src_004; a_021] and five other children (Augustus, Eliza, Charles, Fred, Mary J). This is an original source. Information about the head's name and residence is primary (self-reported to the enumerator); information about children's identities is secondary. Co-residence of William with Henry is indirect evidence of paternity.

### 1880 U.S. Federal Census (Original)

The 1880 U.S. Census (original government enumeration; FamilySearch collection 1417683) enumerates **Willie Bottermiller**, age approximately 17, born 1862 in Minnesota, as a child in the household of **Henry Bottermiller**, age 52, born Germany, farmer, Bertha, Todd County, Minnesota [src_003; a_009, a_012, a_013, a_014]. Also present: wife **Mary Bottermiller**, born approximately 1838 in Germany [src_003; a_016, a_017]. Original source; Henry's name and German origin is secondary information (self-reported); co-residence is indirect evidence of paternity.

### 1885 Minnesota State Census (Original)

The 1885 Minnesota State Census (original government enumeration; FamilySearch collection 1503044) enumerates **William Bottermiller**, born 1862 in Minnesota, in the household of **Henry Bottermiller**, Bertha, Todd County, Minnesota [src_005; a_043, a_049, a_050]. Wife **Mary Bottermiller**, born approximately 1841 in Germany [src_005; a_046, a_047]. Ten additional children present. Original source; corroborates the 1880 picture in full.

### 1929 Oregon Death Certificate (Derivative)

The 1929 Oregon Deaths and Burials database entry for "Henry William Battemiller" — confirmed as William Henry Bottemiller by death date (13 May 1929), place (Clackamas County, Oregon), and surviving spouse ("Lilly Jane") — names the father as "**Casper A. Battermiller**" and the mother as "**Mary Mehlman**" [src_002; a_006, a_007]. This is a derivative source carrying secondary information: the unnamed family informant was not present at William's 1862 birth and was reporting parentage from a 67-year distance.

---

## Conflict Analysis

### C_001: Father's Given Name — "Casper A." vs. "Henry" (RESOLVED)

The 1929 death certificate is the sole source for "Casper A." Three independent original censuses — 1865 MN, 1880 US, 1885 MN — all record the household head as "Henry Bottermiller" (with spelling variants), corroborated by a Minnesota probate witness reference circa 1881 [log_008]. These four independent items were compiled by different enumerators, at different times across twenty years, in different counties and jurisdictions; they share no common informant.

Four concordant independent original records outweigh one derivative record with a distant secondary informant. The death certificate informant was reporting a given name for a man last seen perhaps fifty years earlier. No mechanism exists by which four independent officials across three decades could systematically record the same wrong name. **Resolution: The father's given name is Henry.** "Casper A." is most plausibly an error by the 1929 informant — faulty memory, confusion with a relative's name, or an occasional middle name. I1 has been corrected to "Henry Bottermiller" in tree.gedcomx.json.

### C_002: Mother's Identity — "Anna" (1865) vs. "Mary Mehlman" (1880+) (UNRESOLVED)

The 1865 census names Henry's wife "Anna." The 1880 and 1885 censuses name her "Mary." The death certificate names the mother "Mary Mehlman." Two hypotheses fit:

**(A)** Henry had two successive wives — Anna (first wife, 1865; William's biological mother) died before 1880; Henry then married Mary Mehlman. The 1929 informant named the stepmother as "mother" because she was the only maternal figure they knew.

**(B)** "Anna" in the 1865 index is a transcription error for "Mary" or a similar name, and Mary Mehlman was always William's mother.

Neither can be ruled out without examining the original 1865 register image (ark:/61903/1:2:1G3V-DST, not read in this session). The 1875 Minnesota census was not located; no church records, Anna's death record, or Henry's second marriage record was found. **This conflict remains open.**

---

## Research Scope Note

The 1870 U.S. Census and 1875 MN State Census were not located for this family. The original Oregon death certificate image was not retrieved. The original 1865 census register page was not examined. Minnesota church and immigration records were not found in indexed collections. These gaps are material primarily to the mother's identity question (c_002).

---

## Conclusion

**Tier: Probable.**

The father of William Henry Bottemiller is **Henry Bottermiller** (born approximately 1827–1828, Germany; died after 1885 in Minnesota). Four independent original census records over twenty years, corroborated by a probate witness reference, consistently place William as a child in Henry's household beginning in William's own birthplace just three years after his birth. The sole contradicting source (death certificate naming "Casper A.") is outweighed. This element of the conclusion would stand at the proved tier standing alone.

The biological mother cannot be established with confidence. The 1929 death certificate names **Mary Mehlman** (née Mehlman; born approximately 1838–1841, Germany), and she appears consistently as Henry's wife in 1880 and 1885. However, the 1865 Minnesota State Census — the earliest and most proximate record placing William in a parental household — names Henry's wife as "Anna," not Mary. The most probable interpretation is that Anna was William's biological mother and Mary Mehlman was his stepmother; but this cannot be confirmed without the original 1865 census image and additional records. The mother identification is held at probable pending that further research.

To advance the mother conclusion, the recommended next steps are: (1) image review of the 1865 Minnesota State Census register page (ark:/61903/1:2:1G3V-DST) to confirm or correct the indexed name "Anna"; (2) search for a death record for "Anna Bottermiller" in Minnesota between 1865 and 1880; (3) search for a marriage record for Henry Bottermiller and Mary Mehlman in Minnesota after 1865.
